info.title: Seed Stocks Service - Continuous Analysis Pipeline
info.description: Ranking pipeline and recommendations API. Position execution, capital, and position observability are on **position-tracker-service** (see docs/architecture/seed-vs-position-roles.md).

get.description:
Get top stock recommendations for a trade type.

**Cold start**: When no pre-computed rankings exist (e.g. first deploy, empty universe),
the API fetches symbols from the query service (ARMs), builds universe, runs scoring
(using last-close/candle data when market is closed), then returns recommendations.
If the query returns no symbols, empty list is returned (valid scenario).

**Fast path**: < 50ms when rankings exist (reads from pre-computed ranked_stocks).

**Trade Types**:
- `intraday_buy`: Breakout/momentum (hold until EOD)
- `intraday_sell`: Mean reversion/shorting (hold until EOD)
- `swing_buy`: Pullback in uptrend (2-10 days)
- `short_buy`: Short-term buy trades (1-5 days)
- `long_term`: Long-term trends (30-120 days)

**Response includes**:
- Entry price, stop loss, targets
- Risk/reward ratio
- Entry timing windows (for intraday)
- Reasoning and signals
- Technical snapshot

parameters.name: trade_type

schema.description: Trade type: intraday_buy, intraday_sell, swing_buy, short_buy, long_term
schema.title: Trade Type

parameters.description: Trade type: intraday_buy, intraday_sell, swing_buy, short_buy, long_term

parameters.name: min_score

schema.description: Score floor. Omit to use system setting (api_limits.recommendations_min_score_default). 0 = no floor.
schema.title: Min Score

parameters.description: Score floor. Omit to use system setting (api_limits.recommendations_min_score_default). 0 = no floor.

get.description: Get query registry statistics.

schema.title: Response Get Registry Stats Api V2 Registry Stats Get

get.description: Gainers, losers, top-traded from query + Kite quotes (when market open).

parameters.name: mover_type

schema.description: Bucket: gainers | losers | traded | all (single query, all three ranked lists)
schema.title: Mover Type

parameters.description: Bucket: gainers | losers | traded | all (single query, all three ranked lists)

get.description:
Run full historical performance analysis on stored outcomes.
Returns ARM performance, score bin analysis, indicator correlations,
market regime impact, and time-of-day patterns.
When from_date/to_date are provided, analysis is limited to that time window; otherwise uses last `days`.

parameters.name: from_date

schema.description: Optional start of window (ISO date/datetime, IST)
schema.title: From Date

parameters.description: Optional start of window (ISO date/datetime, IST)

parameters.name: to_date

schema.description: Optional end of window (ISO date/datetime, IST)
schema.title: To Date

parameters.description: Optional end of window (ISO date/datetime, IST)

schema.title: Response Get Performance Analysis Api V2 Analysis Performance Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Full dump of trading + system settings (admin/debug).

**When to use:** Rarely — prefer /trading/form and /system/form for UI to avoid loading unused fields.

**How to use:** GET; no body. For production UI use section-specific GET/PUT below.

**Purpose:** Raw TradingConfig JSON (all sections).

**When to use:** When you need programmatic access to the full config object.

**How to use:** GET; compare with PUT /trading using the same keys for partial updates.

put.description:
**Purpose:** Apply a validated partial patch to trading config (UI saves).

**When to use:** After user edits fields from GET /trading/form.

**How to use:** PUT JSON body: only keys to change. Returns updated config.

get.description: JSON Schema of TradingConfig for UI form generation.

**Purpose:** Primary UI payload: trading sections + field_info + commonly_changed_first.

**When to use:** System Settings page load for trading tab.

**How to use:** GET; render fields from sections; show how_to_use and field_info tooltips.

**Purpose:** Raw SystemSettings: API limits, staleness thresholds, alerts, position_opener times.

**When to use:** Programmatic read of system config.

**How to use:** GET; pair with PUT /system for partial updates.

put.description:
**Purpose:** Validated partial update to system settings (UI saves).

**When to use:** After editing system tab from GET /system/form.

**How to use:** PUT JSON with nested keys matching SystemSettings sections.

get.description: JSON Schema of SystemSettings for UI form generation.

**Purpose:** Primary UI payload for system tab: api_limits, observability thresholds, alerts, position_opener.

**When to use:** System Settings page load for system tab.

**How to use:** GET; use how_to_use + commonly_changed_first to prioritize form fields.

**Purpose:** List registered ARMs (query service scan definitions) with field explanations.

**When to use:** ARM management UI, audits, or choosing a parent when creating a child ARM.

**How to use:** Optional query params: scenario, status, include_inactive. Response includes field_info.

schema.title: Response List Arms Api V2 Arms Get

post.description:
**Purpose:** Persist a new ARM after verify-query succeeds.

**When to use:** UI “Save ARM” after validation; arm_name must be unique.

**How to use:** Body: CreateArmRequest. created_by set to ui.

schema.title: Response Create Arm Api V2 Arms Post

get.description:
**Purpose:** Distinct scenarios and ARM counts for dropdowns.

**When to use:** Before filtering list_arms or when building scenario pickers in UI.

**How to use:** GET only; no params.

schema.title: Response List Scenarios Api V2 Arms Scenarios Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Full ARM record including query_string and child ARMs.

**When to use:** Editing an ARM, debugging scan output, or reviewing hierarchy.

**How to use:** Path: arm_name (uppercase).

schema.title: Response Get Arm Api V2 Arms  Arm Name  Get

put.description:
**Purpose:** Partial update of query, parent, regime, description, or status.

**When to use:** Promote probation→active, fix query after re-verification, or disable an ARM.

**How to use:** PUT with only fields to change; empty patch returns no_change.

schema.title: Response Update Arm Api V2 Arms  Arm Name  Put

post.description:
**Purpose:** Execute a Chartink query via query service; preview symbols (no DB write).

**When to use:** Before POST /api/v2/arms to validate a new or edited query_string.

**How to use:** Body: query_string, widget_id, optional scenario / min_traded_value_rupees for {value_traded_min}.

put.description:
**Purpose:** Assign or clear A/B test group for an ARM.

**When to use:** Setting up an A/B test: assign 'control' to baseline, 'treatment' to variant.

**How to use:** PUT body: {ab_group: 'control'|'treatment'|null}. Null clears the group.

schema.title: Response Set Ab Group Api V2 Arms  Arm Name  Ab Group Put

get.description:
**Purpose:** Compare control vs treatment ARM groups using composite reward (velocity + total return).

**When to use:** After running an A/B test for 7+ days to see if the treatment outperforms.

**How to use:** ?parent_arm=INTRADAY_BREAKOUT&days=30. Returns Welch t-test, p-value, winner.

parameters.name: parent_arm

schema.description: Parent ARM or ARM name prefix
schema.title: Parent Arm

parameters.description: Parent ARM or ARM name prefix

schema.title: Response Ab Analysis Api V2 Arms Ab Analysis Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Find ARMs where entries are better taken in the OPPOSITE direction (buy ARM whose stocks actually move down first → better as sell ARM, and vice versa).

**When to use:** Weekly review to discover misclassified ARMs. A buy ARM with high adverse-before-hit and low max-return is picking stocks that trend down — flip it to a sell scenario.

**How to use:** ?days=30&min_obs=8. Returns ranked list with reason and suggested_scenario.

schema.title: Response Direction Flip Candidates Api V2 Arms Direction Flip Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Find ARMs that outperform their assigned scenario in another scenario.

**When to use:** Periodic review to discover high-velocity ARMs misassigned to wrong scenario.

**How to use:** ?days=30&min_obs=5. Returns ranked list of cross-scenario opportunities.

schema.title: Response Cross Scenario Opportunities Api V2 Arms Cross Scenario Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Show ARM's milestone hit rates (1%,2%,3%,5%,10%), median time-to-hit, and scenario fit scores.

**When to use:** Reviewing which scenario an ARM should be assigned to based on its actual return-speed profile.

**How to use:** ?arm_name=INTRADAY_BREAKOUT&days=30. Returns milestone stats and scenario_fit_scores — highest fit = best scenario match.

schema.title: Response Arm Milestone Profile Api V2 Arms Milestone Profile Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Analyze which indicator ranges (RSI, RVOL, score, distance-to-resistance) yield the best composite reward for an ARM.

**When to use:** Weekly review to tighten Chartink query filters based on outcome data.

**How to use:** ?arm_name=INTRADAY_BREAKOUT&days=60. Returns per-indicator bin analysis + suggested filter changes.

schema.title: Response Improvement Analysis Api V2 Arms Improvement Analysis Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** List symbols seen in ARM universe with latest fundamentals snapshot.

**When to use:** Research UI, screening, or joining candidate data with recommendations.

**How to use:** Optional filters: status, sector, market_cap_category, limit.

schema.title: Response List Candidates Api V2 Candidates Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Observability: counts, status mix, stale fundamentals %, sector breadth.

**When to use:** Dashboards, alerts, or after deploy to verify weekly sync health.

**How to use:** GET; read field_explanations in response for metric definitions.

**Purpose:** One symbol: current row + weekly fundamentals_history (trend arrays).

**When to use:** Deep dive on a single name; chart PE/ROE/margin trends.

**How to use:** Path symbol; optional history_weeks (default 26).

parameters.name: history_weeks

schema.description: How many weeks of history to return
schema.title: History Weeks

parameters.description: How many weeks of history to return

post.description:
**Purpose:** Run the same job as weekly sync immediately (ops/debug).

**When to use:** After fixing Yahoo URL, or to refresh fundamentals before Sunday.

**How to use:** POST no body; returns counts inserted/updated.

put.description: Set status to active, watchlist, or blacklisted.

schema.title: Response Update Status Api V2 Candidates  Symbol  Status Put

get.description:
**Purpose:** List symbols that still use Chartink chart fallback because instrument_token is unset.

**When to use:** After market-movers shows Chartink URLs; prioritize high scan_count names.

**How to use:** GET optional limit; then PUT …/match with instrument_token from Kite instruments CSV/API.

put.summary: Link Chartink / canonical symbol to Kite instrument
put.description:
**Purpose:** Set instrument_token and optional kite_symbol/chartink_symbol aliases on stock_metadata. Propagates exchange + instrument_token to stock_universe and ranked_stocks.

**When to use:** When market movers or scanners return a symbol that matches Kite only after manual mapping.

**How to use:** PUT body JSON; creates a metadata row if missing. Commits with the request session.

put.summary: Fix NSE/BSE exchange for multiple symbols at once
put.description:
**Purpose:** Correct exchange mapping for symbols incorrectly mapped to BSE instead of NSE (or vice-versa). Propagates to stock_universe and ranked_stocks.

**When to use:** When Kite chart URLs show incorrect exchange (BSE vs NSE) for a batch of symbols.

**How to use:** PUT JSON array of {symbol, exchange, instrument_token?}.

get.description: Returns top seed stocks sorted by velocity-aware priority_score. Deduplicates across scenarios so each symbol appears once. Use optimize_for=velocity for fast movers, total_return for magnitude, balanced (default) for both.

post.description:
**Purpose:** Test trading strategies on historical data with realistic costs and slippage

**When to use:** Strategy validation, parameter optimization, performance analysis

**How to use:** POST with date range and strategy parameters, returns detailed performance metrics

get.summary: Quick backtest for last N days
get.description:
**Purpose:** Run a quick backtest for the last N days with default parameters

**When to use:** Rapid strategy testing, recent performance analysis

**How to use:** GET /quick/30 for last 30 days, returns simplified backtest results

**Purpose:** Compare performance of different trade types or score thresholds

**When to use:** Strategy optimization, parameter tuning

**How to use:** GET with multiple trade_types or score thresholds to compare

schema.title: Response Compare Strategies Api V2 Backtesting Compare Strategies Get

get.description: Get current ARM filter configuration and status
Get ARM performance analysis report

schema.title: Response Get Arm Performance Report Api Functional Arms Performance Report Get

post.description: Enable emergency ARM filtering (crisis mode - only top performers)
Disable emergency ARM filtering (back to balanced mode)

get.description: Get top performing ARMs by expected return

schema.title: Response Get Top Performing Arms Api Functional Arms Top Performers Get

get.description: Get currently blocked ARMs with reasons

schema.title: Response Get Blocked Arms Api Functional Arms Blocked Arms Get

post.description:
**Purpose:** Create new ARM variant for manual optimization testing

**When to use:** Manual ARM optimization experiments, parameter testing

**How to use:** POST JSON with parent ARM name, modifications, and test conditions

schema.title: Response Create Arm Variant Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Variants Post

put.description:
**Purpose:** Promote successful ARM variant to production

**When to use:** After A/B testing confirms variant outperforms parent

**How to use:** PUT to promote variant. Optionally replace parent ARM entirely

schema.title: Response Promote Arm Variant Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Variants  Variant Id  Promote Put

post.description:
**Purpose:** Trigger automatic optimization for underperforming ARMs

**When to use:** Scheduled optimization runs or manual trigger for optimization

**How to use:** POST with optional scenario filter. Creates variants for underperformers

schema.title: Response Trigger Auto Optimization Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Auto Optimize Post

delete.description:
**Purpose:** Cleanup old deprecated ARM variants

**When to use:** Periodic maintenance to remove old unused variants

**How to use:** DELETE request removes deprecated variants older than specified days

schema.title: Response Cleanup Deprecated Variants Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Variants Cleanup Delete

get.description:
**Purpose:** Raw ARM optimization configuration including thresholds, automation settings, and alerts

**When to use:** Programmatic read of ARM optimization settings for API integration

**How to use:** GET request returns full configuration. Pair with PUT for updates

schema.title: Response Get Arm Optimization Config Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Get

put.description:
**Purpose:** Update ARM optimization settings with validation

**When to use:** Apply configuration changes from UI or API clients

**How to use:** PUT JSON with nested keys matching ArmOptimizationConfig structure. Partial updates supported

schema.title: Response Update Arm Optimization Config Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Put

get.description:
**Purpose:** JSON Schema for ARM optimization configuration

**When to use:** Generate UI forms or validate configuration programmatically

**How to use:** GET request returns JSON Schema suitable for form generation

schema.title: Response Get Arm Optimization Schema Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Schema Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** UI-friendly ARM optimization configuration with field descriptions and grouping

**When to use:** Load ARM optimization settings page in UI

**How to use:** GET request returns structured configuration with field explanations and commonly changed fields highlighted

schema.title: Response Get Arm Optimization Form Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Form Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** Consolidated learning health: governance, ARM performance, score bins, signal weights, data quality.

**When to use:** Learning observability page. Use ?sections= for lightweight sub-requests.

**How to use:** ?sections=governance,arms&days=30. Omit sections for all.

schema.title: Response Learning Health Api V2 Learning Health Get

get.description: Root health check for load balancers and readiness probes.
Health check

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics

tags.name: Settings (UI)
tags.description: Runtime configuration allowed from the System Settings UI: trading limits, system API limits, staleness thresholds, alerts, and position-opener cutoffs. Prefer GET …/form for field explanations, then PUT with partial JSON patches.

tags.name: Observability
tags.description: Read-only transparency on **this** service: API latency and route inventory (/api/v2/observability/performance), registry stats, candidate coverage. Pipeline DB snapshot and position learning routes live on **position-tracker-service**; see docs/architecture/seed-vs-position-roles.md.

tags.name: Registry — ARMs
